sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to agitate, to shake, e.g. the foot in bood. Psa 68:24 Num 24:8, וְחִצָּיו יִמְחַץ “and he shaketh his arrows,” sc. in blood; compare Ps. loc. cit. (Arab. مخض to agitate, e.g. water in a bucket, see Alb. Schult. De Defectibus Ling. Hebr., p. 75. Origg. Hebr., t. i. p. 100).

(2) to shake, to smite, as any one’s head, Psa 68:22 110:6 the loins, Deuteron. 33:11 . Figuratively, Job 26:12, “by his wisdom he smiteth its (the sea’s) pride,” i.e. he restrains its proud waves. Hence-
